title: chenopodium ambrosioides var. denudatum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides var. denudatum, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a plant species belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and anthelmintic effects. The health benefits of Mexican tea include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, such as bloating, gas, and stomach pain, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever reduction and respiratory issues. Mexican tea can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, and it is also sometimes used as a flavoring agent in traditional Mexican cuisine.
